step_by_step_thinking:
Short sharp pain of tooth transmitted to brain by:
- A: A delta fibers
- B: C fibers
- C: Beta fibers
- D: Gamma fibers To determine through which fiber types short sharp pains travel from teeth to the brain, we must understand the nature of different types of nociceptive signals. Toothaches often involve fast-conducting Aδ fibers because they transmit quick, sharp sensations like those experienced during dental issues or minor trauma. These fibers are myelinated and thus conduct impulses more rapidly than unmyelinated C-fibers.

These fibers are myelinated and thus conduct impulses more rapidly than unmyelinated C-fibers.
\boxed{A}
